description_long: This is a large, four-engine propeller-driven airplane with a robust fuselage and a classic mid-20th-century military bomber silhouette. The wings are straight and mounted at a mid-level on the fuselage, each supporting two engines with prominent propellers. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is positioned at the forward end of the fuselage, with a rounded nose and multiple windows, suggesting a multi-crew configuration. The landing gear appears to be a tricycle type, with main gear under the wings and a nose wheel. The exterior is finished in a dark, metallic color with a glossy sheen, giving it a sturdy and imposing appearance.
description: A large, four-engine propeller airplane with a mid-mounted straight wing, conventional tail, and glossy dark metallic finish. The cockpit is forward-mounted with multiple windows, and the landing gear is tricycle type.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad wingspan, four propeller engines, and the forward cockpit with a rounded nose.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the mid-mounted wings, engine nacelles, and the length of the fuselage.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, as well as the trailing edge of the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the other side of the fuselage, wing, and engine arrangement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units under the wings and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
